# Legal — L1 Capability Map (IT Lens)

**Domain:** Legal
**Industry:** Med-Tech / Healthcare (Abbott Enterprise)
**Lens:** IT
**Date:** 2026-03-16
**Generated by:** Capability Mapper v1.3

> Abbott context applied: Privacy excluded (standalone CPO function), Ethics & Policy excluded (standalone OEC function), Regulatory Affairs excluded (standalone primary domain). BTS is the centralized IT function delivering these capabilities.

---

## Primary Activities
*Technology capabilities that directly enable legal service delivery*

> ⚡ High/Med/Low = digital intensity · 🎯 Edge = differentiating, Core = essential, Commodity = table stakes · 👁 Business/IT/Dual = ownership

**1.** ⚖️ **Matter & Case Management**
Tracks, assigns, and manages all legal matters from intake through resolution — providing lawyers and business clients a single system of record.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**2.** 📄 **Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM)**
Platform-driven management of contract drafting, negotiation, execution, obligation tracking, and renewal across the enterprise.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT  ⚠️ Verify scope — often collapsed into generic Contract Management

**3.** 🔍 **eDiscovery & Litigation Support**
Legal hold management, ESI preservation, data collection, processing, and review platform support for litigation and regulatory requests.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT  ⚠️ Verify scope — often collapsed under Litigation & Dispute Resolution

**4.** 💡 **IP Portfolio & Docketing System**
Platform managing patent, trademark, and IP lifecycle — docketing deadlines, annuity tracking, and prosecution workflow management.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**5.** ⚙️ **Legal Workflow & Process Automation**
Automated workflows for legal intake routing, contract approval chains, compliance training enrollment, and legal hold notifications.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**6.** 🖥️ **Legal Self-Service Portal**
Internal-facing portal enabling business units to submit legal requests, access approved templates, and track matter status without direct attorney engagement.
⚡ High · 🎯 Edge · 👁 Dual

---

## Support Activities
*Technology capabilities that sustain and scale the legal tech ecosystem*

**7.** 📚 **Legal Knowledge Management**
Systems for capturing, organizing, and surfacing legal precedent, playbooks, clause libraries, and institutional knowledge — high GenAI opportunity zone.
⚡ High · 🎯 Edge · 👁 Dual

**8.** 📊 **Legal Analytics & Reporting**
Dashboards providing leadership visibility into matter status, outside counsel spend, risk exposure, and legal function operational performance.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

**9.** 💰 **Legal Spend & Vendor Management**
eBilling platforms, outside counsel performance tracking, billing guideline enforcement, and budget management for all legal vendors and firms.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**10.** 🔐 **Legal Technology Infrastructure & Security**
Hosting, access controls, privileged communication protection, and data security posture underpinning all legal platforms and systems.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**11.** 🔗 **Enterprise System Integration**
Integration and data exchange layer connecting legal platforms with enterprise systems (ERP, HRIS, CRM) for matter, contract, and compliance data flows.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

---

📌 Org Context Note

This map reflects Med-Tech enterprise defaults with Abbott-specific boundaries applied.
Boundaries assumed: Privacy is a standalone CPO function (excluded); OEC is standalone (excluded); Regulatory Affairs is a standalone primary domain (excluded); BTS is the centralized IT function delivering these capabilities.
Adjust if: Your org has embedded divisional IT — Lens assignments for several capabilities would shift from IT to Dual.
Adjust if: Legal Knowledge Management is managed by a dedicated KM team outside Legal — it may belong in a cross-functional KM capability domain.

---

🔎 Completeness Check

Wide scan zones covered (IT lens — all 8):
- Platform & Tooling: ✓ Matter Management, CLM, eDiscovery, IP Docketing
- Data & Analytics: ✓ Legal Analytics & Reporting
- Knowledge Management: ✓ Legal Knowledge Management
- Workflow & Process Automation: ✓ Legal Workflow & Process Automation
- Integration & API Management: ✓ Enterprise System Integration
- Infrastructure & Security: ✓ Legal Technology Infrastructure & Security
- Vendor & Spend Management: ✓ Legal Spend & Vendor Management
- User Experience & Adoption: ✓ Legal Self-Service Portal

Capabilities I considered but excluded:
- Legal Research Tools (Westlaw, LexisNexis) — excluded as vendor subscriptions, not distinct L1 capabilities; absorbed into Knowledge Management
- Compliance Training Platform — excluded per Abbott boundaries; owned by OEC, not Legal

Domain watchlist flags triggered:
- eDiscovery kept separate from Litigation ✓
- CLM kept separate from generic Contract Management ✓
- Legal Knowledge Management kept separate from Legal Operations ✓
- Legal Spend & Vendor Management surfaced via Vendor & Spend zone ✓
- Legal Technology Infrastructure surfaced via Infrastructure & Security zone ✓
- Legal Analytics & Reporting surfaced via Data & Analytics zone ✓

Recommendation: Validate CLM platform ownership at Abbott — if Ironclad/DocuSign CLM is jointly owned by Legal and Procurement, CLM may need to appear on both maps with different accountability scopes.
